text.title: EF Core Data Layer Failure Modes
text.summary: 6 antipatterns observed in production EF Core deployments. Each pairs symptom + root cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Migration apply fails with `Introducing FOREIGN KEY constraint ... may cause cycles or multiple cascade paths` — only at apply time on SqlServer.
antipattern.root-cause: Two relations on the same entity both use `DeleteBehavior.Cascade`; SqlServer rejects the combination.
antipattern.fix: Set `DeleteBehavior.Restrict` or `SetNull` on the secondary FK; review the migration SQL before apply per `migration-sql-review` rule.

antipattern.symptom: Concurrent edits silently overwrite each other; audit log shows "lost update" pattern with no exception.
antipattern.root-cause: Aggregate root has no `RowVersion`/`[ConcurrencyCheck]` field; EF falls back to last-write-wins.
antipattern.fix: Add `[Timestamp] byte[] RowVersion { get; set; }` (SqlServer) or `xmin` mapping (Postgres) on every aggregate root. Catch `DbUpdateConcurrencyException` in the service layer and surface a 409 Conflict.

antipattern.symptom: Endpoint returns 5MB JSON; SQL profile shows a single query returning 10^6 rows for a 1000-row table.
antipattern.root-cause: Multiple `Include().ThenInclude()` chains multiply rows via Cartesian join.
antipattern.fix: Add `.AsSplitQuery()` for collection includes, or project to a DTO with explicit sub-queries.

antipattern.symptom: Login works on SqlServer dev environment, fails on Postgres prod for the same email "Jane@example.com".
antipattern.root-cause: SqlServer is case-insensitive by default; Postgres is case-sensitive. `FirstOrDefault(u => u.Email == email)` differs per provider.
antipattern.fix: Store normalised email (`.ToLowerInvariant()`) and compare normalised on both sides; or use `EF.Functions.ILike` for explicit case-insensitive matching.

antipattern.symptom: Random `InvalidOperationException: A second operation started on this context` under load.
antipattern.root-cause: Author wrapped `await _context.SaveChangesAsync()` in `Task.Run` "to avoid blocking", introducing concurrent DbContext access.
antipattern.fix: Remove `Task.Run` — `SaveChangesAsync` is already async IO. DbContext is not thread-safe.

antipattern.symptom: Each `Configuration` sets `CreatedAt` defaults; three entities forgot it; rows ship with `0001-01-01` timestamps.
antipattern.root-cause: Cross-cutting audit fields configured per-entity instead of via a single interceptor.
antipattern.fix: Add a `SaveChangesInterceptor` that sets `CreatedAt`/`UpdatedAt`/`UpdatedBy` for any entity implementing `IAuditable`. Register once via `optionsBuilder.AddInterceptors(...)`.
